rulings.Case Title: PRABHABEN PATEL VS. CARL F. WILLGEROTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 5, 2011, line 3. Defendants Carl Willgeroth and Beverly Willergoth¿s demurrer to second amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The court cannot now say that there is no reasonable possibility that plaintiff can state a claim against these defendants. However, plaintiff is admonished that this may be his last opportunity to try to allege a viable claim against these defendants. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AARON KUMLIN et al VS. BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 5, 2011, line 5. Defendants¿ demurrer to third amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligence, overruled as to the 2(b) cause of action for intentional infliction, and taken off calendar as to the first (erroneously labeled the fourth) cause of action for assault. The negligence cause of action is barred by the ministerial exception, while the intentional infliction claim is predicated in part upon the alleged assault which is outside the ministerial exception. Defendants withdrew their demurrer as to the assault claim. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN HEALY VS. JRD HOLDINGS INC., DBA RESTAURANT DEPOT LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 5, 2011, line 20, DEFENDANT NORTHWEST CONVERTING CO., DBA PREMIER MOP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Northwest Converting Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Because defendant did not move for summary adjudication, it must prevail on both claims alleged against it in order for its motion to be granted. There are triable issues of fact whether defendant owed a duty sounding in negligence to the plaintiff. See Defendant Depot¿s responses to plaintiff¿s facts 8, 12, 14 and 16. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)
